details: Starting DPRINT-level triage of moreh_nll_loss Reg B regression. HEAD c443f7d757f. 19 fail / 51 pass / 60 skip; failures span reduction=mean AND reduction=sum-fp32_dest_acc_en=True-weight=Yes. Implementer fix attempts neutral. Hypothesis to investigate: init_bcast BIG-init emission per-tile in BinaryFpu clash chain corrupts engine state.

Forward shape=[5,10] mean+weight: torch=-0.4012 ttnn=0.0544 (wrong sign & magnitude). MaxATOL=0.456. Reproduces cleanly on smallest WEIGHT+DIVISOR forward shape. Sign flip strongly suggests Negative (T1.28 chain) result lost or applied to wrong intermediate.

After running backward_compute_kernel_options with --run-all: all failures correlate with reduction_mean=True (DIVISOR branch). reduction_mean=False (sum) all PASS regardless of fp32/weight. Forward reduction=sum+weight also passes. Briefing claim of sum-fp32-weight failure was inaccurate. Strengthens hypothesis that bcast scalar (init_bcast BIG init) per-tile is the root cause.

H1 (init_bcast BIG-init per-tile corruption) DISPROVEN. Swapped in mains pristine pre-migration step2_kernel.cpp (no eltwise_chain at all, pure raw LLK as on main HEAD). Test STILL FAILS with same Max ATOL Delta=0.37 magnitude. This means the regression is NOT in the migrated kernel — its in some other change on the branch (likely tt_metal LLK headers, eltwise_unary.h, or bcast.h). Migration commit 6ea995dc66d is NOT the cause.

H2: Bug is shape-dependent. shape=[5,10] FAILS (small, 1 tile, 5/1024 valid), shape=[5,50,2,7,50,70] PASSES (many tiles, junk averaged). Test isolates fine — deterministic failure. cb_divisor_recip[0,0]=0.0678 implies divisor=14.7, but expected divisor=sum-of-valid-weights=1.1335 (computed from torch ground truth). The divisor coming into step2 is wrong — by ~13x. Bug is upstream (step1+moreh_sum) or in padding handling, not in step2 itself. PRE-MIGRATION kernel fails identically — this is NOT caused by the migration.

ROOT CAUSE: moreh.sum is broken on this branch for small/edge shapes. Verified directly: moreh.sum([0.2, 0.7, 0.1, 0, 0]) returns inf (expected 1.0). Larger shapes work (e.g. [64,32] of ones returns 2048 correctly). The bug is in moreh_sum (probably reduce_helpers_compute.inl additions on commits a5b493a70f9, bc1e2208419, 1b3c648e72d, or transitive) — NOT in moreh_nll_loss kernels. moreh_nll_loss test fails because moreh.sum is called twice (once for divisor in MEAN reduction, once for final loss), and the divisor becomes inf, causing 1/inf=0, then anything*0=0, output=0. Migration commit 6ea995dc66d is COMPLETELY INNOCENT — applying mains pristine pre-migration moreh_nll_loss_step2_kernel.cpp produces the SAME failure (Max ATOL=0.40121, tt_loss=0.0). Test isolation confirmed with --run-all suite showing pattern: ALL failures correlate with reduction_mean=True (which uses divisor=moreh_sum(...)). sum/none reduction passes (no divisor required).

ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: moreh.sum returns ±inf for 1D inputs on this branch (independent of moreh_nll_loss migration). Verified with proof script REGB_root_cause_proof.py: shapes [5], [16], [32], [100] all produce inf; 2D shapes [5,32], [16,16], [32,32], [32,16] all correct. moreh_nll_loss(reduction=mean) calls moreh.sum on target_shape (a 1D tensor) → divisor=inf → 1/inf=0 → loss=0. Implementer fix attempts disproven hypothesis was indeed correct: migration is innocent. Pre-migration kernel produces same delta. Likely suspect: tt_metal/hw/inc/api/compute/matmul.h reordering of mm_init LLK calls on this branch interacts with moreh_sum_w (which uses matmul-based reduce). But moreh_sum_w uses mm_init_short — which is unchanged. The actual fix needs deeper investigation by the implementer (out of expert-debugger scope).
